hero.sub: A strong community starts with representation rooted in experience, listening, and action.

about.title:
A life dedicated
to her community
about.p1: Hélène Boudreau has been a registered nurse for over 22 years and a committed resident of Dieppe. Her professional journey has taught her the importance of listening, rigour, and compassion — values she naturally brings to her civic engagement.
about.p2: A volunteer with the Carnaval de l'Amitié, the Aquatic Centre, and Chair of the Maison Nazareth Board of Directors, she knows intimately the strengths and challenges of our region.

distinction.title: Woman of the Year
distinction.p: Hélène Boudreau was honoured with the Woman of the Year award for her exceptional commitment to the Dieppe community. This distinction reflects a remarkable journey of service, combining leadership, dedication, and concrete impact on citizens' lives.
distinction.quote: True leadership means being there — before, during, and after.

items.text: Coming soon — Hélène Boudreau will share her vision for a dynamic and engaged Dieppe.

items.title: Dieppe in Health
items.text: Coming soon — Concrete commitments for the well-being and health of Dieppe residents.

items.title: Dieppe in Evolution
items.text: Coming soon — A modern and ambitious vision for the future of our community.

items.title: Housing
items.text: Accelerate construction of affordable housing, revise zoning policies, and support homeownership for families.

newcomers.title: Issues
newcomers.sub: Because a strong city is an open city.
newcomers.p1: Dieppe is one of the fastest-growing cities in Atlantic Canada, largely thanks to its newcomers. Hélène Boudreau is committed to building bridges, facilitating integration, and ensuring every resident feels at home.
newcomers.p2: Her commitments: multilingual access to services, support for settlement organizations, and equitable representation on city council.

community.title:
Actions,
not just words
community.sub: Years of presence on the ground, alongside the residents of Dieppe.

vote.hero_sub: Find out who can vote in the 2026 Dieppe municipal election and how to register on the electoral list.
vote.who_card1_p: To vote in Dieppe's municipal election, you must:
vote.who_card1_li2: Be at least 18 years old on election day
vote.who_card1_li3: Have resided in the City of Dieppe for at least 40 days before election day
vote.who_card2_p: For more information on Dieppe-specific requirements and New Brunswick municipal elections:
vote.who_card2_link: Municipal Elections Guide — Government of N.B.
vote.how_card1_p: Make sure you are registered on the electoral list before election day. If you are not registered, you can do so in person at the elections office or online.
vote.how_card2_p: On voting day, you must present a photo ID with your address, or two pieces of identification without a photo, one of which shows your current address.
vote.how_card3_p: You will receive a voter information card indicating where and when to vote. Polling stations are generally open from 10 a.m. to 8 p.m. on election day.
vote.how_card4_p: If you cannot vote on election day, advance polling stations will be available. Dates and locations will be officially announced by Elections NB.

don.page_title: Make a Donation — Hélène Boudreau, Dieppe 2026
don.sub: The online donation form will be available very soon. Thank you for your generosity and support for Hélène Boudreau's campaign for Mayor of Dieppe.
